name: Houthis (Yemen)
summary: Day 86 (Monday). **A4 RED SEA NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW DAY 7 OF 7 CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — no second incident confirmed since May 11 Hodeidah; UKMTO recent-incidents page shows no new advisory through Monday morning IST; MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement. May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of new pattern. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase** (NYT preparation May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu call May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17) **is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern** when Houthi attacks compounded with US escalation rhetoric. Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. Investor read: Iran signalling escalation control, not escalation. **PRIOR (May 17): B4 HOUTHIS RED SEA — NO CLAIMED OR UNCLAIMED MARITIME INCIDENT THROUGH MAY 17 MORNING IST. Negative-space holds Day 6 of 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah.** No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST. The May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident still stands as a single event — no second Red Sea attack within the 7-day window opened. Project Freedom pause Day 12; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. **The Houthi non-action persists through the weekend during which Trump-Iran is publicly hardening — Houthi quiet during a US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not a coincidence.** If no incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. **PRIOR (May 14):** Day 82. A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE: Day 3 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed explosion — UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page shows NO second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). If no second incident occurs by ~May 18 (Day 7), the May 11 incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than start of a new pattern. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61 (no fresh redirect actions). The Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains the operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak per JPost + CNN intel — Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling. PRIOR (May 13): UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km (80 mi) southwest of Hodeidah on Monday May 11; the ship continued voyage with no injuries, and the vessel was briefly 'not under command' (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO advisory primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim responsibility — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** The non-claim is itself a signal: possibilities include (i) deniable Houthi action (Iran-aligned signal without attribution), (ii) Houthi action awaiting domestic framing, (iii) unrelated incident. This represents a potential break of the cycle's most stable non-event (80+ days of zero Red Sea attacks during Iran's multi-axis kinetic restart). **If a second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses; if not, this remains 'single incident, unclaimed.'** Until then: pattern undeclared, sample size 1. UN Security Council Resolution 2812 (2026) framework (monthly reporting through July 15) remains structurally intact pending attribution. Prior coordination-decay framework spanned Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, Project Freedom + pause cycle, May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, May 8 US tanker strikes, May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach. Persistent VLCC/Frontline rate elevation remains HORMUZ-driven, NOT Bab el-Mandeb-driven.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis begin confirmed Red Sea attacks on NEUTRAL commercial shipping (verified by maritime trackers) — Day 47 attacks RESUMED but ONLY on belligerent-linked vessels (US/UK/Israeli). Neutral shipping
Houthis launch confirmed attack on Israel (missile/drone reaching Israeli territory)

recent_signals.headline: **A4 RED SEA DAY 7 OF 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW CLOSES UNTRIPPED — May 11 Hodeidah reverts to 'single unclaimed event'** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO recent-incidents page primary, MARAD 2026-006 stands without supplement). The trigger 'Second Red Sea shipping incident within 7 days of May 11 Hodeidah-area UKMTO advisory' on strait-of-hormuz node moves watching → untripped_expired. **Houthi non-action during a publicly-hardening US kinetic-preparation phase is the cleanest behavioural divergence from the March-April pattern**: NYT preparation report May 16, Trump 'Calm Before the Storm' May 16, Trump 'clock is ticking' May 17, Trump-Netanyahu phone call May 17, Israeli security cabinet May 17, Saudi drone interception May 17, UAE Barakah drone strike May 17. The houthis→red-sea and red-sea→strait-of-hormuz edges carry forward at high weight but the *absence* of action under a multi-vector US escalation cycle is operationally consistent with Houthi command being instructed by Iran to hold fire. **Pair with TASS/Xinhua non-amplification of Trump posts and Mojtaba Day 11 of state-media silence** — coordinated quietism across Iran's proxy and patron networks against US kinetic noise. The investor read: Iran is signalling escalation control, not escalation, against Washington's posturing. The non-action read is structurally validated through one full negative-space cycle.
sources: UKMTO recent-incidents page (operational primary, no new advisory through Monday IST)
Gard Insights 'Red Sea update: Resumption of Houthi campaign' (industry assessment)
recent_signals.verification: Day 7 untripped = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO operational primary, MARAD official; multi-day window closes); coordinated quietism reading = CONFIRMED analytical observation (negative-space + Mojtaba silence + TASS/Xinhua restraint all corroborate).

If no second incident by ~May 18 (Day 7), May 11 reverts to 'single unclaimed event' rather than pattern start. CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. Houthi non-claim of May 11 remains operative signal. Iran missile fire rate collapsed 92% from Feb-Mar peak (JPost + CNN intel) = Iran-aligned proxy network operating well below historic kinetic ceiling.

recent_signals.verification: No second Red Sea incident through Day 3 of 7-day window = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (UKMTO primary); CENTCOM redirect tally held = CONFIRMED ABSENCE-OF-CHANGE.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 81 — HOUTHI RED SEA NON-EVENT POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11. UKMTO reported multiple explosions near a vessel ~130 km SW of Hodeidah; vessel briefly 'not under command' but continued voyage, no injuries (CONFIRMED ACTION — UKMTO primary, Times of Israel corroborator). **Houthis did NOT claim — attribution UNCONFIRMED.** First Red Sea shipping incident with possible Houthi attribution since Iran's May 4 kinetic restart, breaking the 80-day quiet that had spanned Iran's multi-axis restart + Project Freedom cycles + Hezbollah cross-border reach. The non-claim is itself a signal: deniable Houthi action, action awaiting framing, or unrelated incident. **Trigger window: if second Red Sea incident occurs within 7 days of May 11, the Houthi non-action read collapses.** Until then: single incident, sample size 1, pattern undeclared. Coordination-decay framework not yet falsified but under first material test in 80 days.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary multi-source) + UNCONFIRMED attribution (Houthi non-claim; no party has publicly claimed)

recent_signals.headline: DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET — NO HOUTHI ATTACKS ON COMMERCIAL SHIPPING IN PAST 24 HOURS; UNSCR 2812 MONTHLY REPORTING FRAME INTACT (NEXT REPORT JULY 15). UKMTO advisories continue to flag heightened threat level but no incidents. Houthis reiterated rhetorical solidarity with Iran across early-cycle messaging but operational silence is now in 80th consecutive day. Houthi non-response now spans (a) Iran's May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart, (b) US Project Freedom + pause cycle, (c) the May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, (d) the May 7-8 second day of US tanker strikes, (e) the May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach (corrected to adjacent territory), AND now (f) the Sunday May 10 bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike. The full proxy-restraint pattern is multi-event confirmed: even when other Iranian proxies (Hezbollah) demonstrate first cross-border reach AND when an unclaimed strike occurs near a fellow GCC mediator's coast, Houthis remain quiet. The non-action remains the signal.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED NON-ACTION (intelligence in the absence) — UKMTO + MARAD + Lloyd's List multi-source

historical_summaries.summary: 10 signals. Abdul Malik al-Houthi declared group 'prepared to enter the war' with 'fingers on the trigger' in recorded address | Mohammed al-Bukhaiti (political bureau member, not military commander) told NYT: 'the expansion of the conflict to include other countries, | Three weeks into war, no Houthi attacks

22 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | CONFIRMED ABSENCE: Day 34 of war — ZERO confirmed attacks on merchant shipping in Red Sea or Bab el-Mandeb | Day 35: ZERO confirmed Red Sea shipping attacks
